root_text.sn35.164:1.3: “Yo kho, koṭṭhika, anattā tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.5: Cakkhu kho, koṭṭhika, anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.6: Rūpā anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.7: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.8: Cakkhusamphasso anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.9: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.164:1.10: jivhā anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.164:1.11: mano anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.12: Dhammā anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.15: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.164:1.16: Yo kho, koṭṭhika, anattā, tatra te chando pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.164:1.3: “Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is not-self.
translation_text.sn35.164:1.8: and eye contact are not-self: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.164:1.9: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also not-self: You should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.164:1.15: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also not-self: you should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.164:1.16: Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is not-self.”
